reasoning: Enterobiasis (pinworm infection) is highly treatable with cheap and effective oral anti-helminthic medications (like albendazole and mebendazole) and causes relatively mild symptoms. There is virtually zero commercial or global public health drive to develop a vaccine for this specific infection. Furthermore, there are currently no approved vaccines for any human helminths, and developing a vaccine typically takes many years of clinical trials. A web search reveals no current late-stage vaccine candidates in clinical trials for enterobiasis. Consequently, the probability of a vaccine being developed, approved, and added to the Wikipedia article 'List of infectious diseases' within the exceedingly short timeline (up to February 2026) is near zero. I am bounding the probabilities at the allowed minimum of 0.05, increasing slightly for later resolution dates to reflect inherently greater uncertainty over longer horizons.
